title: Studio Roadmap
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Records the verified completed path, current approved reconciliation work, controlled execution queue, and long-term direction of TMS-OS.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document records the approved strategic direction of the Two Marshalls Studios Operating System. It separates completed foundations, current approved work, the controlled execution queue, deferred ideas, and long-term studio direction.

ROADMAP-001 is a strategic control document. STATE-001 records the current operating checkpoint, ARCH-001 records the implemented architecture, historical documents preserve completed work, and ENH-LOG-001 preserves unapproved improvements.

sections.title: Roadmap Control Rules
items: Only verified completed work may be listed as complete.
Only explicitly approved work may appear in the active execution queue.
Repository file existence does not by itself authorize execution or prove a module is operational.
Current-state detail belongs in STATE-001; architecture detail belongs in ARCH-001.
Historical sequence belongs in WS-HIST-001, MILE-HIST-001, HIST-001, and ARCH-EVO-001.
Unapproved enhancements belong in ENH-LOG-001 and must not interrupt approved work.
A new active milestone requires an explicit decision after the current milestone reaches its completion standard.

sections.title: Long-Term Studio Direction
paragraphs: TMS-OS is intended to become the internal command center for every major Two Marshalls Studios function while preserving clear governance, recoverability, and controlled change.

The long-term direction includes studio governance, documentation, development, game production, assets, publishing, marketing, business operations, knowledge management, and future approved automation.
items: Maintain one governed studio operating environment.
Preserve project knowledge independently of chat history and any single workstation.
Support repeatable work sessions with traceable decisions, tests, file changes, and closure.
Provide governed permanent documentation with human approval and rollback protection.
Support studio development and game-production planning.
Support asset, publishing, marketing, and business operations through shared architecture.
Introduce automation only through approved, testable, reversible implementation.

sections.title: Verified Completed Foundation Path
paragraphs: The following foundation capabilities are verified by the uploaded repository and recovered project history. Detailed dates and session-level history remain governed by the permanent history documents.
items: Headquarters dashboard and shared studio navigation
Department pages for Documentation, Development, Assets, Publishing, Marketing, and Business
Shared visual and component architecture
StudioDB foundation, database engine, modular data loading, and record architecture
Documentation catalog, structured JSON documents, shared document template, and embedded viewer
Shared search and cross-department search presentation
Session Context Engine and persistent work-session context
Session Manager, Prepare Session review, and human approval-controlled closure
Document Update Engine and generic permanent-document writer framework
Document Writer Registry
Permanent writers for WS-HIST-001, STATE-001, DOC-STATE-001, DEC-LOG-001, and MILE-HIST-001
Permanent Transaction Manager
Rollback Package Generator and Original Document Capture foundation
Transaction planning, dependency, readiness, manifest, integrity, checksum, and deterministic signature-proposal modules
Controlled-output, authorization, approval, verification, rollback, lifecycle, and state-transition module foundations
Permanent-document inventory and repository reconciliation audit

rows: Disabled unless later controlled verification proves otherwise

sections.title: Current Active Milestone
paragraphs: Documentation Reconciliation
The current milestone aligns the core permanent documents with the verified repository while preserving established architecture, governance, and history.
The milestone does not authorize redesign of stable systems or creation of additional permanent-document categories without evidence of an unowned governance responsibility.

rows: Installed and visually verified; final session validation pending
Historical and register documents through the reconciliation checkpoint
Permanent-document catalog, viewer, search, registry, and console validation
Select and approve the next software milestone from verified roadmap and history evidence

sections.title: Core Documentation Reconciliation Scope
items: STATE-001 — approved current operating state
ARCH-001 — verified current repository architecture
ROADMAP-001 — approved strategic direction and execution queue
DOC-STATE-001 — documentation health and reconciliation status
DOC-INDEX-001 — permanent-document inventory and control index
STD-001 and PROC-001 through PROC-004 — governance standards and operating procedures
WS-HIST-001, MILE-HIST-001, DEC-LOG-001, HIST-001, ARCH-EVO-001, BUG-HIST-001, and LESSON-001 — verified history
RISK-001, TECH-DEBT-001, and ENH-LOG-001 — current controlled registers

sections.title: Documentation Reconciliation Completion Standard
paragraphs: The current milestone may close only when the core permanent documents accurately reflect the repository, their responsibilities do not conflict, required history is preserved, and the Studio Portal can load, render, search, and catalog the reconciled documents without new errors.
Completion also requires a controlled session review and explicit human approval. A document may be installed for testing without being permanently accepted.
items: Core document content reconciled against repository evidence
Structured JSON validity confirmed
Document Viewer rendering confirmed
Search behavior confirmed
Catalog metadata confirmed
Browser Console contains no new reconciliation-related errors
Git change scope contains only approved files
Session review package prepared
Human approval recorded before commit and push
Current and historical records updated consistently

sections.title: Post-Reconciliation Decision Gate
paragraphs: No next software module is automatically activated by completing documentation reconciliation.

After the milestone closes, the verified permanent roadmap, current state, milestone history, architecture map, enhancement log, and repository must be reviewed together. Stephen must then approve one next milestone and one first module.
Until that decision is recorded, the next software milestone remains Pending Selection rather than invented.

sections.title: Verified Strategic Development Areas
paragraphs: The repository and recovered roadmap establish the following long-term development areas. They are strategic direction, not automatically active commitments.
items: Studio Portal and shared command-center capabilities
Documentation and studio knowledge management
Project and task management
Game-development management
Dead Roads design and production records
Quest, asset, and build tracking
Publishing operations
Marketing operations
Business operations
Approved AI and automation capabilities

sections.title: Future Milestone Framework
paragraphs: Future milestones must be selected one at a time after reconciliation. The following framework preserves known strategic direction without declaring an unsupported implementation order.
items: Studio Operations Management — project, task, assignment, progress, and notification capabilities
Game Production Management — game design, quest, asset, build, and release tracking
Publishing Operations — publishing workflows and release support
Marketing Operations — campaign, content, and performance support
Business Operations — studio administration and operating controls
Knowledge and Automation — governed recommendations, research, AI assistance, and approved automation

sections.title: Deferred Governance Proposals
paragraphs: The WS086 audit did not establish a current need to create the following separate permanent documents. Their responsibilities are already fully or partially covered by the existing document system.
They remain deferred proposals and may be reconsidered only after a specific unowned responsibility is demonstrated.
items: VISION-001
CONSTITUTION-001
PROGRAMS-001
CATALOG-001
GOVERNANCE-TRANSITION-001

sections.title: Deferred Enhancement Relationship
paragraphs: ENH-LOG-001 is the permanent holding location for future improvements that are not part of the current approved work.
An enhancement becomes active roadmap work only after explicit approval, dependency review, roadmap placement, and selection as part of a controlled milestone.

sections.title: Work That Must Not Interrupt the Current Milestone
items: Unrelated feature development
Unapproved architecture expansion
Activation of permanent writes or rollback execution
Creation of duplicate governance documents
Convenience improvements not required for reconciliation
Unapproved automation
Deferred ideas already controlled by ENH-LOG-001

sections.title: Roadmap Decision Gates
items: Gate 1 — Is the proposed item supported by repository evidence, permanent records, or an explicit approved decision?
Gate 2 — Does the item belong in the roadmap rather than STATE-001, ARCH-001, history, or ENH-LOG-001?
Gate 3 — Are dependencies and safety boundaries known?
Gate 4 — Is the current milestone complete and approved before a new milestone begins?
Gate 5 — Has one first module been selected without silently activating the entire future program?
Gate 6 — Are testing, documentation, rollback, session closure, commit, and push requirements defined?

sections.title: Current Next Action
paragraphs: Complete controlled installation and verification of ROADMAP-001, then reconcile DOC-STATE-001.

After the core control documents are aligned, reconcile DOC-INDEX-001, procedures, standards, history, and registers. Do not resume feature development until the Documentation Reconciliation completion standard is met and the milestone is explicitly closed.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The Studio Roadmap preserves the approved path of Two Marshalls Studios by separating completed foundations, current controlled work, future strategic direction, and deferred ideas.

TMS-OS will move forward one approved milestone and one controlled module at a time, using repository evidence, permanent documentation, testing, human approval, recoverability, and explicit session closure.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent roadmap to the shared structured Document Template Engine.

Reconciled the roadmap with the verified repository, established Documentation Reconciliation as the active milestone, defined the controlled reconciliation queue, and separated strategic direction from current state and architecture.

verification.method: Reconciled against the uploaded studio-portal repository, VERSION.md, the existing permanent roadmap, the legacy repository roadmap, PERMANENT-ARCHITECTURE-MAP.md, and the approved WS086 project state.
